title: Jenkins Insurance Full Demo Site + Complete Admin Panel (Frontend)

requirements.summary: Build the full Jenkins Insurance Agency public-facing demo site by composing all Jenkins-branded section components
acceptanceCriteria: All 11 sections render correctly in sequence on the public page
Sticky nav transitions background on scroll
Hero headline and agent photo animate on load
Trust bar count-up animations trigger on scroll into view
WhatsApp floating button has pulse animation
Mobile sticky bar shows Call Now and WhatsApp buttons
All 6 city names appear in footer service areas
Phone (281) 410-8934 appears in header and contact section

file_operations.description: Update to compose all 11 Jenkins section components in correct sequence: JenkinsHeader (sticky), JenkinsHero (full-screen with animations), JenkinsTrustBar (gold bar with count-up stats), JenkinsCarrierBadges (grid), JenkinsWhyChoose (cards), JenkinsCoverageTypes (grid), JenkinsAbout (C. Jenkins section), JenkinsReviews (Google reviews), JenkinsQuoteContact (form), JenkinsBilingual (Spanish section), JenkinsFooter (with Agent Login link), JenkinsFloatingWhatsApp (floating button), JenkinsStickyMobileBar (mobile sticky bar). Set document title to 'Jenkins Insurance Agency | Greater Houston TX' on mount. Ensure all IntersectionObserver-based scroll animations are enabled.

Update sticky header to transition background on scroll using scroll event listener. Display phone number (281) 410-8934 prominently in header. Use navy #1B3A6B and gold #F4B942 colors. Ensure mobile hamburger menu works correctly.

Implement full-screen hero with animated headline using fade-in/slide-up effect on mount. Add float animation effect for agent photo using the workspace image frontend/public/assets/generated/agent-photo-jenkins.dim_400x500.png. Include two CTA buttons and trust indicators panel. Use navy/gold color scheme with Montserrat for headings.

Implement gold (#F4B942) trust bar with four count-up stats that animate when scrolled into view using IntersectionObserver. Stats: families insured, star rating, carrier partners, location. Implement number animation that counts up from 0 to target value over 2 seconds.
Display grid of insurance carrier partner badges with hover highlight effects. Add scroll-triggered fade-in animation using IntersectionObserver. Use white background cards with subtle shadows.
Render three value-proposition cards explaining why to choose Jenkins Insurance. Add hover scale animations and scroll-triggered fade-in effect using IntersectionObserver. Use navy/gold color scheme.

Display 6 coverage type cards in 3-column grid (home, auto, business, flood, RV, life) with icons and descriptions. Add hover animations and scroll-triggered visibility using IntersectionObserver. Each card includes a 'Get Quote' action that scrolls to the quote form.

Render About C. Jenkins section with agent photo (use workspace image frontend/public/assets/generated/agent-photo-jenkins.dim_400x500.png), bio text, and stats bar showing years, clients, carriers, and rating. Add intersection-observer-triggered fade-in animation. Use navy/gold styling.

Display customer reviews section with three testimonial cards and Google rating summary (4.9 stars, 127 reviews). Use IntersectionObserver to trigger fade-in animations on scroll. Read reviews from localStorage (key: 'jenkinsReviews') with fallback to hardcoded default reviews.

Implement quote/contact form collecting name, phone, email, city (dropdown of 6 cities), coverage type, and message. Submit via useSubmitQuote hook. Display phone (281) 410-8934 and contact info prominently. Show success state after submission. Use navy/gold styling with Open Sans font for body text.

Render Spanish bilingual section promoting services for Texas families with phone CTA button. Add scroll-triggered fade-in animation using IntersectionObserver. Read Spanish text from localStorage (keys: 'jenkinsBilingualHeadline', 'jenkinsBilingualBody', 'jenkinsBilingualButton') with fallback to hardcoded Spanish defaults. Use gold accent color.

Update footer to display three columns: brand/contact info, quick navigation links, and service areas (list all 6 cities: The Woodlands, Spring, Humble, Magnolia, Tomball, Conroe). Add 'Agent Login →' text link that navigates to /admin route. Include social icons and copyright bar with dynamic year. Use navy background with white/gold text.

Implement fixed floating WhatsApp button in bottom-right corner with tooltip on hover and pulse animation defined in index.css. Link to pre-filled WhatsApp message for Jenkins Insurance. Use gold (#F4B942) background.

Create mobile-only fixed bottom bar (hidden on desktop) with two action buttons: phone call link (281) 410-8934 and WhatsApp chat link. Use navy and gold colors with white text. Position fixed at bottom with z-index above content.

Ensure CSS custom properties define Jenkins brand colors: --jenkins-navy: #1B3A6B, --jenkins-gold: #F4B942, --jenkins-white: #FFFFFF. Import Montserrat for headings and Open Sans for body text from Google Fonts. Define scroll-fade, pulse (for WhatsApp), and float animations used throughout the Jenkins components.

requirements.summary: Add 'Agent Login →' link to JenkinsFooter navigating to /admin
acceptanceCriteria: Footer displays 'Agent Login →' text link
Clicking it navigates to /admin route

file_operations.description: Add 'Agent Login →' link in footer that navigates to /admin using React Router Link component. Style as inline gold text link with hover underline effect. Position in a visible location within the footer layout.

requirements.summary: Create /admin login page with password authentication using sessionStorage
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to /admin shows the login page when not authenticated
Correct password (admin123) redirects to /admin/dashboard
Wrong password shows error message
Authentication state persisted in sessionStorage

file_operations.description: Create admin login page component with centered card layout using navy/gold styling. Display 'Jenkins Insurance Admin Panel' title (use workspace image frontend/public/assets/generated/jenkins-logo.dim_300x80.png for logo), password input field, and 'Login' button. Implement password validation (demo password: 'admin123'). On correct password, store 'adminAuthenticated' = 'true' in sessionStorage and redirect to /admin/dashboard using useNavigate. On incorrect password, show inline error message. Use navy card with gold button and white text.
Register /admin route that renders AdminLoginPage component (no layout wrapper, no sidebar/topbar). Ensure /admin is publicly accessible without authentication check.

requirements.summary: Create full admin panel layout with sidebar, top bar, and protected routes
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar is 260px wide on desktop, navy background
All 9 menu items visible with emoji icons
Active route menu item is highlighted gold
Top bar shows 'Welcome, C. Jenkins', current date, bell with red dot, 'View My Website →' button
Mobile: sidebar collapses and opens via hamburger
Unauthenticated access to any /admin/* route redirects to /admin

file_operations.description: Create admin layout component with: (1) Fixed left sidebar 260px wide, navy (#1B3A6B) background, containing 9 menu items with emoji icons: 📊 Dashboard, 📋 Quote Requests, ⭐ Reviews Manager, ✏️ Edit Website Content, 📞 Contact Info Settings, 🌆 Service Areas, 📈 Analytics, ⚙️ Settings, 🚪 Logout. Active menu item highlighted with gold (#F4B942) text or background using useLocation to detect active route. (2) Top bar with white background showing 'Welcome, C. Jenkins', current date, notification bell icon with red dot badge, and gold 'View My Website →' button linking to '/'. (3) Main content area with white background and scroll, rendering child routes via Outlet. (4) Mobile: sidebar collapses to hamburger drawer with smooth transition animations. Implement authentication check: if sessionStorage 'adminAuthenticated' is not 'true', redirect to /admin.

Wrap all /admin/* child routes (dashboard, quotes, reviews, content, contact, service-areas, analytics, settings) in AdminJenkinsLayout. Ensure /admin itself (login page) is NOT wrapped in layout. Implement route protection in layout component that checks sessionStorage for authentication.

requirements.summary: Build Admin Dashboard page with stat cards, recent quote requests table, and quick actions
acceptanceCriteria: 4 stat cards render with correct values, colors, and icons
Recent Quote Requests table shows all 5 sample rows
Status badges are correctly color-coded
Each row has View (navy) and Mark as Contacted (gold) buttons
Quick Actions card shows all 4 buttons
Dashboard is the default page after login

file_operations.description: Create dashboard page with: (1) Top row of 4 stat cards: Card 1 (navy): icon 📋, value '12', label 'Quote Requests This Month', subtext '↑ +3 from last month' (green). Card 2 (gold): icon ⭐, value '4.9', label 'Google Rating', subtext '127 total reviews'. Card 3 (green): icon 💰, value '$4,200', label 'Est. Revenue This Month', subtext 'Based on 12 leads × avg policy'. Card 4 (navy): icon 👁️, value '847', label 'Website Visitors This Month', subtext '↑ +23% from last month' (green). (2) Below cards: 'Recent Quote Requests' table with columns Name, City, Coverage, Date, Status showing 5 sample rows (Sarah M./The Woodlands/Home+Auto/Today/New, Robert T./Humble/Flood/Yesterday/Contacted, Maria G./Spring/Business/Mar 1/Quoted, Jennifer K./Magnolia/Auto/Feb 28/Contacted, David L./Tomball/Life/Feb 27/Closed). Each row has navy 'View' button and gold 'Mark as Contacted' button. Status badges color-coded: yellow=New, green=Contacted, blue=Quoted, green checkmark=Closed. (3) Right side: 'Quick Actions' card with 4 buttons: '+ Add New Review' (gold), 'Edit Phone Number' (navy), 'Update Business Hours' (navy), 'Download All Leads CSV' (navy). Use hardcoded sample data.
Register /admin/dashboard route rendering AdminJenkinsDashboard component within AdminJenkinsLayout. Set as default redirect after login from AdminLoginPage.

requirements.summary: Build Quote Requests page with filter bar, sortable table, and detail modal
acceptanceCriteria: Filter bar renders all 5 filter controls plus Export CSV button
CSV export downloads leads data as a .csv file
Table shows all columns and is sortable by clicking headers
Status badges are color-coded correctly
Delete shows confirmation dialog before removing
View modal shows all fields including editable notes, status dropdown, WhatsApp, Email, and Mark Closed buttons
Lead data persists in localStorage

file_operations.description: Create quote requests page with: (1) Filter bar containing search input (by name/email), City dropdown (6 cities), Coverage Type dropdown, Status dropdown, date range inputs, and gold 'Export to CSV' button that generates and downloads CSV file of lead data. (2) Sortable table with columns: #, Name, Phone, Email, City, Coverage, Message, Date, Status, Actions. Make headers clickable to sort by column. Status badges: yellow=New, green=Contacted, blue=Quote Sent, green checkmark=Closed, red X=Not Interested. Actions per row: View (opens modal), Change Status (inline dropdown), Add Notes, Delete (with confirmation dialog using window.confirm or custom modal). (3) Pre-populate with 8+ sample leads including the 5 from dashboard. (4) View modal popup showing full lead details, editable Notes textarea, Status dropdown, green 'Send WhatsApp' button (opens wa.me link), navy 'Send Email' button (opens mailto), gold 'Mark Closed — $749 Won!' button. Store all lead data in localStorage (key: 'jenkinsQuoteLeads'). Implement CSV export using Blob and URL.createObjectURL.
Register /admin/quotes route rendering AdminJenkinsQuotesPage component within AdminJenkinsLayout.

requirements.summary: Build Reviews Manager page with add/edit/delete/reorder functionality
acceptanceCriteria: Reviews render as cards with all required fields
Add New Review form opens with all fields including star selector
Edit and Delete work with confirmation on delete
Move Up/Down reorders reviews
Overall rating auto-calculates and updates when reviews change
Data persists in localStorage

file_operations.description: Create reviews manager page displaying: (1) Overall Rating display that auto-calculates average of all reviews and updates live. (2) Gold '+ Add New Review' button at top opening form (inline or modal) with: clickable 1-5 star rating selector, Reviewer Name input, Reviewer City dropdown (6 cities), Review Text textarea, Date input, 'Save Review' gold button. (3) Current reviews as cards showing star rating (filled stars), review text, reviewer name, city, date. Each card has: edit icon (✏️) opening edit form, delete icon (🗑️) with confirmation dialog, move up (⬆️) and move down (⬇️) buttons for reordering. (4) Pre-populate with 3+ sample reviews. Store all review data in localStorage (key: 'jenkinsReviews'). Ensure JenkinsReviews public component reads from same localStorage key so changes appear immediately on live site. Implement star rating selector as clickable 5-star row that highlights on hover and click.
Register /admin/reviews route rendering AdminJenkinsReviewsPage component within AdminJenkinsLayout.

requirements.summary: Build Edit Website Content page with expandable sections for all editable content
acceptanceCriteria: All 5 expandable sections render with their respective fields
Each section has Save Changes (gold) and Preview Changes (navy) buttons
Saving stores data to localStorage
Public site Jenkins components read from localStorage and reflect changes
Success toast appears bottom-right after saving
Default values shown when localStorage is empty

file_operations.description: Create content editor page with 5 expandable accordion/card sections: (1) HERO SECTION: main headline input, subheadline input, button 1 text input, button 2 text input, 4 trust indicator text fields. (2) ABOUT SECTION: agent name input, about text textarea, years experience number input, families served number input, carriers count number input. (3) CONTACT INFO: phone number input, email address input, physical address textarea, Mon-Fri hours input, Saturday hours input, WhatsApp number input. (4) COVERAGE TYPES: title + description inputs for each of 6 coverage cards (home, auto, business, flood, RV, life). (5) BILINGUAL SECTION: Spanish headline input, Spanish body text textarea, Spanish button text input. Each section has gold 'Save Changes' button and navy 'Preview Changes' button. Save to localStorage with keys: 'jenkinsHero', 'jenkinsAbout', 'jenkinsContact', 'jenkinsCoverage', 'jenkinsBilingual'. Show success toast '✅ Saved successfully!' bottom-right after save (use shared toast context). Preview button opens public site in new tab. Use accordion component for collapsible sections.
Register /admin/content route rendering AdminJenkinsContentPage component within AdminJenkinsLayout.

requirements.summary: Build Analytics page with stat cards and charts
acceptanceCriteria: 4 top stat cards render with sample data
Monthly Quote Requests bar chart shows correct 6-month data with gold bars
Leads by Coverage Type pie/donut chart shows 5 segments with correct percentages
Leads by City shows horizontal bars with correct city names and percentages
All charts are responsive

file_operations.description: Create analytics page with: (1) Top row of 4 stat cards: Total Visitors This Month, Total Quote Form Submissions, WhatsApp Button Clicks, Phone Number Clicks (use sample numbers). (2) Bar chart 'Monthly Quote Requests' showing 6 months (Oct=4, Nov=6, Dec=5, Jan=8, Feb=10, Mar=12) with gold (#F4B942) bars on white card background (border-radius 16px, padding 32px). Implement using Recharts BarChart component. (3) Pie/donut chart 'Leads by Coverage Type': Homeowners 35%, Auto 28%, Bundle 20%, Business 10%, Other 7%. Implement using Recharts PieChart with innerRadius for donut effect. (4) Horizontal bar chart 'Leads by City': The Woodlands 32%, Spring 24%, Humble 20%, Magnolia 12%, Tomball 8%, Conroe 4%. Render as CSS progress bars with gold fill and city labels. Use hardcoded sample data for all charts. Ensure responsive layout with charts stacking on mobile.
Register /admin/analytics route rendering AdminJenkinsAnalyticsPage component within AdminJenkinsLayout.

requirements.summary: Build Settings page with profile, notifications, website, and password sections
acceptanceCriteria: All 4 settings sections render with correct fields
Notification toggles are gold when on, defaulting to all enabled
Toggle states persist to localStorage
Update Password validates that new password and confirm password match
Success toast appears after saving each section

file_operations.description: Create settings page with 4 sections: (1) PROFILE: Agent Full Name input, License Number input (label: 'TX Dept of Insurance'), Agency Name input, Profile Photo upload button (file input). (2) NOTIFICATIONS: 4 toggle switches (gold when on, defaulting to all enabled): 'Email me when new quote arrives', 'WhatsApp alert for new leads', 'Weekly summary email', 'Monthly analytics report'. (3) WEBSITE: Custom domain text field (placeholder 'yourname.com'), 'Connect Domain' button, Google Maps embed URL field, Facebook Page URL field, LinkedIn Profile URL field. (4) CHANGE PASSWORD: Current password input, New password input, Confirm password input, 'Update Password' navy button with validation that new and confirm match. Each section has its own save button. Persist notification toggles and other settings to localStorage (keys: 'jenkinsProfile', 'jenkinsNotifications', 'jenkinsWebsite'). Show success toast after saving each section. Use gold toggle switches when enabled, gray when disabled.
Register /admin/settings route rendering AdminJenkinsSettingsPage component within AdminJenkinsLayout.

requirements.summary: Build Contact Info Settings and Service Areas stub pages
acceptanceCriteria: Contact Info Settings page renders with phone, email, address, hours fields
Service Areas page shows 6 cities with checkboxes
Both pages are accessible from sidebar navigation
Changes persist to localStorage

file_operations.description: Create contact info settings page with editable fields: phone number input, email address input, physical address textarea, Mon-Fri hours input, Saturday hours input, WhatsApp number input. Add gold 'Save Changes' button. Save to localStorage (key: 'jenkinsContactInfo', same as used in Content page). Show success toast after save. Pre-populate from localStorage or use defaults matching JenkinsFooter contact info.

Create service areas page showing list of 6 cities (The Woodlands, Spring, Humble, Magnolia, Tomball, Conroe) with checkboxes to enable/disable each. Add 'Add City' text input and 'Add' button to append new cities. Add gold 'Save Changes' button. Save enabled cities array to localStorage (key: 'jenkinsServiceAreas'). Show success toast after save. Default all 6 cities to enabled.
Register /admin/contact route rendering AdminJenkinsContactPage and /admin/service-areas route rendering AdminJenkinsServiceAreasPage, both within AdminJenkinsLayout.

requirements.summary: Implement global toast notification system for admin panel
acceptanceCriteria: Toast appears bottom-right after any save action
Toast auto-dismisses after ~3 seconds
Toast has a green/navy style consistent with brand
All save buttons show a loading spinner while saving

file_operations.description: Create toast context with ToastProvider component and useToast hook. Implement toast state management with showToast(message, type) function. Toast component appears fixed at bottom-right, auto-dismisses after 3 seconds, and supports success/error/info types. Success toast has green checkmark icon and navy/gold styling. Exports ToastProvider to wrap admin layout and useToast hook for components.
Wrap layout content with ToastProvider so all admin pages can access toast notifications via useToast hook.
Add loading state to save buttons (show spinner while saving). Call showToast('✅ Saved successfully!', 'success') after successful save using useToast hook.
Add loading state to save buttons. Call showToast('✅ Saved successfully!', 'success') after adding/editing review.
Add loading state to all save buttons. Call showToast('✅ Saved successfully!', 'success') after saving each section.
Add loading state to save button. Call showToast('✅ Saved successfully!', 'success') after saving.
Add loading state to save button. Call showToast('✅ Saved successfully!', 'success') after saving.

requirements.summary: Update all Jenkins public site components to read content from localStorage with fallbacks
acceptanceCriteria: JenkinsHero reads headline, subheadline, button text from localStorage
JenkinsAbout reads agent name, bio, stats from localStorage
JenkinsBilingual reads Spanish text fields from localStorage
JenkinsCoverageTypes reads card titles/descriptions from localStorage
JenkinsReviews reads review list from localStorage
All components fall back to hardcoded defaults when localStorage keys are absent

file_operations.description: Read hero content from localStorage (key: 'jenkinsHero') containing main headline, subheadline, button 1 text, button 2 text, and 4 trust indicators. Fall back to hardcoded default values when localStorage is empty. Use useState with lazy initialization reading from localStorage on mount.

Read about content from localStorage (key: 'jenkinsAbout') containing agent name, about text, years experience, families served, and carriers count. Fall back to 'C. Jenkins' and hardcoded defaults when localStorage is empty.
Read bilingual content from localStorage (key: 'jenkinsBilingual') containing Spanish headline, body text, and button text. Fall back to hardcoded Spanish defaults when localStorage is empty.

Read coverage types from localStorage (key: 'jenkinsCoverage') containing title and description for each of 6 coverage cards. Fall back to hardcoded defaults (home, auto, business, flood, RV, life) when localStorage is empty.

Read reviews array from localStorage (key: 'jenkinsReviews') containing review objects with star rating, text, name, city, date. Fall back to 3 hardcoded default reviews when localStorage is empty. Auto-calculate overall rating from review array.
Read contact info from localStorage (key: 'jenkinsContactInfo') for phone, email, address, hours. Fall back to (281) 410-8934 and hardcoded defaults when localStorage is empty.

Read service areas from localStorage (key: 'jenkinsServiceAreas') for city list and contact info from localStorage (key: 'jenkinsContactInfo') for phone/address. Fall back to 6 default cities and (281) 410-8934 when localStorage is empty.

requirements.summary: Register all admin routes with authentication protection
acceptanceCriteria: All 9 admin routes are registered and accessible
Unauthenticated access to protected routes redirects to /admin login
After login, user lands on /admin/dashboard
Logout clears sessionStorage and redirects to /admin login

file_operations.description: Register all admin routes: /admin (public, renders AdminLoginPage with no layout), /admin/dashboard (AdminJenkinsDashboard), /admin/quotes (AdminJenkinsQuotesPage), /admin/reviews (AdminJenkinsReviewsPage), /admin/content (AdminJenkinsContentPage), /admin/contact (AdminJenkinsContactPage), /admin/service-areas (AdminJenkinsServiceAreasPage), /admin/analytics (AdminJenkinsAnalyticsPage), /admin/settings (AdminJenkinsSettingsPage). All routes except /admin are wrapped in AdminJenkinsLayout which implements authentication check. Set /admin/dashboard as default redirect after login.

Add logout button in sidebar that clears sessionStorage key 'adminAuthenticated' and redirects to /admin using useNavigate. Ensure authentication check runs on mount and redirects to /admin if not authenticated.
